steps.title: Pinpoint the defining signal
steps.content: The conclusion claims invasives are “advantaged by delays” in subfreezing. Advantage means the invasive must show a more favorable response to this temperature change than the native species.

steps.title: Identify the differential response
steps.content: Choice A shows it directly: when freezing is delayed, P. aviculare extends leaf production much longer than V. edule, extending the invasive's growing season. The other three either show no differential response to temperature timing, reverse the advantage, or confound the effect with other seasonal variables. That matches choice A — A is correct.
